1.title: A version control tool is used to manage the codebase

2.title: Argo Data accessed locally by the software are assumed to be organised following the GDAC folder structure
2.evidence: Argopy local access to Argo data assume a GDAC structure

3.title: Argo Data accessed remotely by the software are retrieved from an official GDAC server (http, ftp, s3 servers)
3.evidence: Argopy support all official GDAC servers

4.title: Argo Data used by the software are traceable with a persistent identifier (Argo GDAC DOI or snapshot DOIs)
4.evidence: Argo DOIs is preserved in dataset returned by Argopy

5.title: Argo Meta-Data accessed remotely by the software are retrieved from an official Argo sources (GDAC ftp, GDAC https, NVS, SparQL, STAC)
5.evidence: Meta data are always retrieved from official servers

6.title: Argo papers and DOIs are referenced in the software metadata

9.title: Data produced by the software respect Argo file formats and conventions wherever relevant (e.g. NetCDF CF-1.7)
9.evidence: Argopy does not produce official netcdf files

16.title: Metadata produced by the software follow Argo conventions (as in reference tables provided by the NVS server)
16.evidence: Argo conventions are respected wherever relevant

17.title: Non-Argo Data used by the software are explicitly identified and traceable with a persistent identifier (DOI) when available

18.title: Non-Argo Data used by the software should be accessible online
18.evidence: Non-Argo data can be downloaded by the library and are accessible online

21.title: The codebase includes comments and is documented following referenced standards (e.g. numpy style docstrings in Python)

22.title: The codebase includes comments and is documented to explain the purpose and functionality of each module, class, and function
22.evidence: Argopy uses docstrings to document the codebase internally

23.title: The codebase is "consistent", i.e. it follows consistent naming conventions, coding styles, and design patterns throughout the software
23.evidence: We use black and flake8 to implement this guidelines

34.title: The project includes a LICENSE file

35.title: The project includes a README file

36.title: The project includes a basic documentation

39.title: The project is hosted on a web-based development platform (e.g., GitHub or GitLab).

40.title: The project is hosted on an Argo-recognised web-based development platform

41.title: The software documentation and codebase are written in English

47.title: The software documentation includes usage guidelines and examples
47.evidence: See README and full documentation at https://argopy.readthedocs.io

50.title: The software has been presented to the Argo community (e.g. Data Management Team meeting)
50.evidence: Argopy was presented to the Argo community during the ADMT24 and ASW7 meetings

56.title: The software is released under an open-source license

57.title: The software provides a clear description of its dependencies

58.title: The software uses a programming language adopted within the Argo community (e.g. Matlab, Python, R, Java, Julia)

59.title: The software uses an open-source programming language (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_open-source_programming_languages)
